subject: Add file conflict detection to orchestration and SKILL.md
description:
Add pre-wave file conflict detection to prevent concurrent agents from editing the same files within a wave. Implement in both orchestration.md (detailed procedure) and SKILL.md (workflow step).

**What to implement:**

1. Add conflict detection procedure to `claude/sdd-tools/references/orchestration.md`:
   - Pre-wave scan: parse all wave tasks' `description` and `acceptance_criteria` for file path references
   - File path detection patterns:
     - Paths containing `/` (e.g., `src/api/handler.ts`)
     - Paths ending in known extensions: `.md`, `.ts`, `.js`, `.json`, `.sh`, `.py`
     - Glob patterns (e.g., `src/api/*.ts`)
   - Conflict flagging: two or more tasks reference the same file path
   - Resolution: lower ID task stays, higher ID tasks deferred to next wave (artificial dependency inserted)
   - Overlapping glob patterns treated as conflicts
   - Log results in `execution_plan.md` under "Conflict Resolution" section

2. Update `claude/sdd-tools/skills/execute-tasks/SKILL.md` (~271 lines):
   - Add conflict scan step to wave planning workflow (between dependency sort and wave launch)
   - Reference orchestration.md procedure for details

**Files to modify:**
- `claude/sdd-tools/references/orchestration.md` (~611 lines)
- `claude/sdd-tools/skills/execute-tasks/SKILL.md` (~271 lines)

**Acceptance Criteria:**

_Functional:_
- [ ] Pre-wave scan detects file path references in task descriptions and acceptance criteria
- [ ] Paths with `/`, known extensions, and glob patterns are detected
- [ ] Two tasks referencing same file â†’ conflict flagged
- [ ] Lower ID task stays in wave, higher ID deferred
- [ ] Conflict resolution logged in execution_plan.md
- [ ] SKILL.md includes conflict scan step in workflow
- [ ] No conflicts â†’ wave proceeds unchanged with no overhead

_Edge Cases:_
- [ ] No file paths in descriptions â†’ no conflicts detected
- [ ] All tasks conflict on same file â†’ sequentialized (one per sub-wave)
- [ ] Glob patterns overlap â†’ treated as conflict
- [ ] File path only in acceptance criteria (not description) â†’ still detected

_Error Handling:_
- [ ] Path pattern regex fails â†’ log warning, proceed without detection for this wave

**Testing Requirements:**
â€˘ Manual: Verify conflict detection patterns match spec examples
â€˘ Integration: Validated during end-to-end execution session (Task 15)

Source: internal/specs/execute-tasks-hardening-SPEC.md Section 5.1
